mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market and robust economic activity, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns. The signal is based on the 3-month moving average of monthly changes.
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market strength is a key input for NBER business cycle dating.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in PAYEMS shows a negative rank correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is near the zero lower bound, monetary policy is perceived as ineffective for stimulating a deeply damaged economy. This signals persistent economic weakness and causes negative equity returns as investors anticipate lower growth and earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:ZIRP policy was implemented in Dec 2008 in response to the GFC.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% for a given month.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in both retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals a broad-based contraction in consumer and investment demand. This joint weakness leads to sharply lower earnings expectations and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS < 0 AND 3-month change in HOUST < 0
evidence_refs: fact:Housing and consumption collapsed concurrently during the 2008 GFC.
failure_condition: An interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST changes is not statistically significant (p > 0.1) in a regression predicting 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates robust manufacturing and supply-side activity, translating to higher corporate revenues and positive short-term equity market sentiment. This is a core, always-on indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: The month-over-month change in INDPRO has a rank correlation below 0.03 with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate from its recent lows signals a sharp economic downturn is underway, triggering investor risk aversion and negative equity returns as recession fears mount. This is a variant of the Sahm Rule.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment, an accommodative monetary policy is unsustainable and signals a future policy error. This conflict between rising CPI and low FEDFUNDS creates uncertainty and leads to negative returns as investors anticipate future aggressive tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:Stagflationary periods of the 1970s showed this dynamic.
failure_condition: The model's hit rate does not improve when adding the interaction term compared to models with only CPIAUCSL or FEDFUNDS individually.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth is the broadest measure of economic activity. Positive quarter-over-quarter changes signal an expanding economy, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity returns over a medium-term horizon.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter percentage change in GDPC1 is not positively correlated with subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns over the full sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Unexpectedly high inflation prints increase the probability of imminent monetary tightening by the Federal Reserve. This raises the discount rate for equities and causes negative, rapid market reactions as future rate hikes are priced in.
evidence_refs: fact:High market sensitivity to CPI release dates is well-documented.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).
